title: Exception audit round 2, data-driven: fix top offenders by observed frequency in silent_failures.db
current_state: The repo has ~288 swallowed-exception sites in 64 files. Round 1 (T-254/T-255) instruments the top 20 in the two user-facing hotspots. The next biggest by static count: tools/tools_memory.py (16 - though several are intentional Supabase-offline guards), tools/tools_browse.py (12), scripts/retro.py (12 - partial data is acceptable there by design). Static counts cannot tell which sites actually fire in production; after round 1 lands, data/silent_failures.db (via agent/observability.py track_silent + the silent_failure_watcher passive) accumulates real frequencies.
target_state: After 1-2 weeks of round-1 telemetry: the top offenders BY OBSERVED COUNT get either a real fix (if the failure is fixable) or instrumentation + user-visible fallback (if it is a legitimate degradation path). Sites that never fire are left alone - that is the whole point of doing this data-driven instead of a blind 288-site mega-audit.
migration_plan: 1. Query data/silent_failures.db grouped by site, ordered by count, since round-1 landing date.

2. For each of the top ~10: read the site, classify - real bug (fix it), legitimate degradation (instrument + fallback message), intentional guard like Supabase-offline (leave, add a one-line comment naming the intent).
3. Fix/instrument accordingly; each real bug found here gets its own reproducing test.
5. If the db is near-empty after 2 weeks, close this ticket with that finding - silence in telemetry is a legitimate resolution.
risk_notes: Do not start before telemetry exists - the ordering IS the deliverable. tools_memory.py guards need extra care: several swallows are deliberate offline-resilience (Supabase down must not kill a turn); classify before touching.
